short: Jens Biegert is ICREA Professor of Attoscience and Ultrafast Optics at ICFO. A pioneer of mid-infrared photonics and attosecond soft X-ray science, his work has produced new ways to image chemical reactions, track electron dynamics in molecules, and observe carrier motion in quantum and condensed-matter systems. He is a Fellow of the Studienstiftung des deutschen Volkes, a Fellow of the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation, a Fellow of Optica, a Fellow of the American Physical Society, and an elected member of the Academia Europaea.

p: He received his PhD in physics from the Technische Universität München, based on research conducted at the University of New Mexico under the supervision of Jean-Claude Diels. Following his doctorate, he pursued his habilitation at ETH Zürich, where he established a research programme in attosecond science.
His work focuses on the generation, control, and application of ultrashort laser pulses to probe and manipulate matter on electronic and atomic timescales.
Since joining ICFO, Biegert has been a pioneer of mid-infrared photonics and attosecond soft X-ray science, driving advances in laser technology and ultrafast measurement techniques.

His research has enabled new approaches to imaging chemical reactions, tracking electron dynamics in molecules, and observing carrier motion in quantum and condensed-matter systems. He has also made key contributions to laser-induced single-electron diffraction and attoscience, providing tools for resolving quantum dynamics with atomic spatial and attosecond temporal resolution.
He currently coordinates a European Commission FET consortium, participates in two further FET projects, and is a Guest Professor at the Fritz Haber Institute of the Max Planck Society in Berlin.

He served on Optica's Board of Directors and as Chair of its Meetings Council, and contributed to its Strategic Planning and Finance Councils. He served as Executive Director of Laserlab-Europe, uniting 46 major laser infrastructures across 22 European countries, and as a Board Member of ARIE, representing large-scale analytical research infrastructures across Europe on behalf of some 40,000 researchers.
